question_text: Let X1, X2, . . . , Xn be a random sample from a distribution with cumulative

options.D: istribution function F(x). Let the empirical distribution function of the sample be Fn(x). The classical Kolmogorov-Smirnov goodness of fit test statistic is given by Tn = √nDn = √n sup −∞<x<∞ |Fn(x) −F(x)|.
